title: hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. burserianum
intro: Hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. burserianum, also known as Burserian hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and southern France. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. burserianum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. burserianum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
